Azure SQL dominance: 4 of 5 matched resource groups (80%) include both microsoft.sql/servers and microsoft.sql/servers/databases, indicating Azure SQL is the preferred relational backend; plan to provision an Azure SQL logical server and database as the default relational component for the finance web app.

Hyperscale tendency for databases: the most common database SKU observed is HS_Gen5 (40% of database instances), suggesting existing databases favor Hyperscale Gen5 configurations; plan to evaluate Hyperscale (Gen5) vCore sizing and cost trade-offs when selecting the database tier for the finance workload.

Key Vault adoption: microsoft.keyvault/vaults appear in 60% of matched resource groups, showing a pattern of using Key Vault for secrets and certificates; plan to integrate Azure Key Vault for DB credentials and TLS artifacts and design secure access (preferably via managed identities) rather than embedded secrets.

Observability is common: microsoft.insights/components (Application Insights) and microsoft.alertsmanagement/smartdetectoralertrules appear in 60% of matched RGs and operationalinsights/workspaces in 40%, indicating telemetry and alerting are standard practices; plan to include Application Insights and Log Analytics with alerting in the deployment baseline for the finance app and database.

Perimeter-focused network posture with no private endpoints: 60% of RGs include network security perimeter resources while VNet integration and private endpoints are absent across observed resources (0/76), indicating reliance on perimeter controls rather than internal network isolation; plan to evaluate adding VNet integration or private endpoints and NSGs for the web app and Azure SQL if the finance app requires stronger internal-only access controls.

Low managed identity usage: only 8 of 76 resources (11%) have managed identities enabled, indicating limited current use of identity-based resource access; plan to adopt system- or user-assigned managed identities for the web app and service-to-KeyVault/DB access to reduce secret management and improve security posture.

Regional concentration: eastus contains the largest share of resources (57% of observed resources), indicating a regional preference in the estate; plan to default deployments to eastus for operational consistency and latency alignment unless finance-specific data residency or compliance constraints require a different region.

App Service sizing pattern: microsoft.web/sites is present in 40% of matched RGs and the most common serverfarm SKU observed is S1 (33% of serverfarms), suggesting App Service is used with conservative sizing; plan to consider Azure App Service for hosting but validate and likely right-size the App Service plan (above S1) to meet production performance and availability needs for the finance application.
